raw:
{
  "judge_id": "responsibility-bearing judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept of statelessness and its contradiction with the sliding-window histograms, and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., the headline number attributed to the wrong component), and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, including uncertainty disclosure about reconstructed ablation splits and inserted specifications.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., underspecified terms).",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept of statelessness and its contradiction with the sliding-window histograms, and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., the headline number attributed to the wrong component), and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, including uncertainty disclosure about reconstructed ablation splits and inserted specifications.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., underspecified terms).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not annotated with source and reason.
